caption: best omakase ive had no doubt #japan #travel
transcript: Rating everything I did in Japan and if it was worth it or not. Let's get to it. Eating omakase. If I had one word to describe this omakase experience, I would say it was unserious, but in a good way. For context, we literally had the chef and his team come out in Mario Brothers costumes and grate a ton of truffle onto our dishes at some point. Music was playing, everyone was cracking jokes, the vibes were immaculate. Very stark difference to the omakase experience I had in the past where it was very intimate, very quiet, very serious. And honestly, I feel like this just made it 10 times better and way more enjoyable. And honestly, the food was even better too. That uni, you paid around $125 per person, 10 out of 10 experience and 100% worth it. Taking a day trip to Mino Falls in Osaka. So when you get there, it's around a 30 to 40 minute hike from the beginning of the trail to the waterfall. The road is completely paved the whole way through so it's super easy, anyone can do it. You're pretty much just walking through nature and trees the whole time, it's beautiful. And at the end of it, you're greeted with a gorgeous, gorgeous waterfall. Amazing place to take pictures and just take in the scenery. There's people serving food. There's ice cream vending machines. You already know I copped a cool-ish. It was a 10 out of 10 experience and 100% worth it. Getting a custom embroidered denim jacket at Levi's Osaka. I'm not sure if other stores do this, but at Levi's Osaka, when you buy a denim jacket, they have an embroidery station you can take your jacket to. They have a bunch of pre-made designs or you can bring in your own custom designs and they can print it in any size on pretty much anywhere on the jacket. The only downside is you do usually have to wait a day before it's done, so you do have to go back, which does take time, so I docked some points for that. The embroidery and size that I picked ended up being around 1100 yen, which is super cheap. Overall, I'd give that experience an 8.5 out of 10. I will say it's worth it though because you do end up with a one-of-one piece that'll last you a lifetime. That's pretty much it from my list. If you have any suggestions for cool Japan experiences that I should do on my next trip, be sure to let me know in the comments down below. Peace.
